key_insight: Neural network depth is adapted by treating training as an optimal control problem and inserting layers where a posteriori error is largest.
engineering_relevance: The method gives FEM-style error control for surrogate models, helping scientific ML architectures target complexity where it matters most.
method: Formulate network weights and biases as piecewise linear controls over depth.
Use dual-weighted residual a posteriori estimates to bound functional error.
Refine architecture by adding layers at intervals with the highest estimated contribution to error.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical tests on scientific datasets, including an observable-to-parameter map for the Navier–Stokes equation, with better generalization than existing adaptation methods.
watch_item: The approach depends on reliable error estimation and adds algorithmic complexity, so practical performance may be sensitive to estimator quality and implementation overhead.
paper_title: An optimal control approach for neural network architecture adaptation with a posteriori error estimation
